metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 6 e
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Employer, breeder, applicant, holder, assignee, licensee, representative, and brand roles remain distinct. Historical employment was not treated as current employment, ambiguous names were left unresolved, and no external communications were made.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact string is not a uniquely identifiable Spanish legal entity. 'S.L.' is a legal-form suffix and 'Hortalizas' is a generic business term; exact-name searches did not establish a company, individual, public institution, or rightsholder. Similar company names, including longer names containing both terms, were not treated as matches.
profiles.leadershipSummary: No leadership, succession, employer, or ownership relationship can be assigned without a complete legal name or another disambiguating record.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Unresolved by design. Do not merge this string with Frutaria Comercial de Frutas y Hortalizas, S.L. or any other horticultural company without record-level evidence.

profiles.ownershipType: public-institution research administrator and historical fruit breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Resolved as Yoshiki Kashimura (樫村芳記). NARO records credit him on public fruit-breeding work, including the Japanese pear 'Rinka'; the reviewed career record places him at NARO and predecessor fruit-tree institutes before a move to the Bio-oriented Technology Research Advancement Institution (BRAIN) in 2021. Breeder credit does not establish personal title, and the reviewed cultivar evidence is institutional NARO work.
profiles.headquarters: Kawasaki, Kanagawa, Japan (BRAIN worksite); historical NARO fruit-tree research in Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: The latest reviewed career record identifies Kashimura as a BRAIN research director from April 2021, managing publicly funded R&D after earlier fruit cultivation, postharvest, and breeding work at NARO. This is a transition from hands-on public research to public research administration, not evidence of a personally owned breeding program.
profiles.webResearchNotes: The romanized database order is surname-first. Cultivar names are portfolio evidence, not trademark claims. No current private-company ownership or personal licensing authority was found.

profiles.ownershipType: retired public-university extension specialist and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Kallsen is a retired University of California Agriculture and Natural Resources farm advisor and a co-breeder of UC pistachio cultivars. Patent evidence for 'Gumdrop' identifies The Regents of the University of California as assignee; it does not show personal ownership. Cultivar licensing and commercialization therefore belong on the UC institutional path.
profiles.headquarters: California, United States; historical UC Cooperative Extension service in Kern County and UC Davis pistachio program affiliation
profiles.leadershipSummary: UC records state that Kallsen retired in 2023. The expanded UC Davis Pistachio Breeding Program is now led by Patrick J. Brown and Giulia Marino, with material from the Parfitt-Kallsen program continuing under the university team.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Farm Advisor Emeritus (Retired), UC Agriculture and Natural Resources
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public-university breeder credit is distinct from Regents ownership. The listed email is a public emeritus directory contact, not a representation that Kallsen controls licensing.

profiles.ownershipType: historical public-institution fruit breeder and later industry-association executive
profiles.ownershipSummary: Resolved as Tetsuo Masuda, a breeder in NARO and predecessor apple and pear programs. NARO publications credit him on cultivars and rootstocks, while a 2013 institutional publication records his then-current affiliation with the Japan Fruit Tree Seedling Association. The reviewed evidence supports institutional cultivar ownership and association employment, not personal title.
profiles.headquarters: Historical NARO apple research in Morioka, Iwate; later Japan Fruit Tree Seedling Association affiliation in Tokyo, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Masuda moved from public fruit breeding into a senior industry-association role by the early 2010s. No sufficiently current official source was found to assign a 2026 role or personal successor; the NARO breeding program itself continued through a multi-researcher institutional team.
profiles.webResearchNotes: The latest located affiliation evidence is historical and is labeled as such. Cultivar names are examples of credited program work, not proof of surviving rights or trademarks.

profiles.ownershipType: corporate berry-breeding director and second-generation breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Marionnet Label identifies Laurent Chausset as director of CRIV and as a breeder following his father, Daniel Chausset. Its official history places variety creation in Marionnet Label, plant sales in Pépinières de Sologne, and licensing and distribution in Earth Market after a 2020 restructuring; Marionnet Label is presented as the R&D division of Agri Finest Company. No reviewed source establishes personal title to the cultivars.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official team page presents Chausset as the current CRIV director and second-generation breeder. It also identifies junior breeder Nolwenn Andouard as developing into a key future team member, providing a public succession-bench signal within a breeding team of more than ten people.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official CRIV team and history pages identify Chausset as the breeding director and place the breeding program in Marionnet Label within Agri Finest Company. This supports a company affiliation, but it is not evidence that every record carrying his breeder name has the same legal holder.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Official pages support the displayed variety and brand examples. They do not establish the registered trademark owner for each name, so no registry-level trademark conclusion is made.

profiles.ownershipType: corporate strawberry breeder without verified personal title
profiles.ownershipSummary: Patent records identify Iana Kostina as an inventor on a continuing series of Driscoll's strawberry cultivars and identify Driscoll's, Inc. as applicant or assignee. The evidence supports an active corporate breeding affiliation through recent filings, but not personal ownership, an independent company, or personal licensing authority.
profiles.headquarters: Watsonville, California, United States (Driscoll's corporate program)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Recent filings show Kostina working in a multi-inventor Driscoll's breeding team. No public personal management title or individual succession event was established; portfolio continuity sits with the strategic-scale corporate program.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Multiple strawberry patent records identify Kostina as inventor and Driscoll's as applicant or assignee. This establishes corporate affiliation for those records, but does not justify automatically rolling up every name-only breeder record without matching title data.
profiles.webResearchNotes: The DrisStraw names are patented cultivar denominations, not asserted here as consumer trademarks. Public business contact routes to Driscoll's rather than to the employee personally.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Resolved as Frederick G. Gmitter Jr., professor of citrus genetics and breeding at the University of Florida/IFAS Citrus Research and Education Center. Patent and licensing records place cultivar rights and commercialization with institutional entities, including Florida Foundation Seed Producers, Inc.; breeder status does not establish personal ownership.
profiles.headquarters: UF/IFAS Citrus Research and Education Center, 700 Experiment Station Road, Lake Alfred, Florida 33850, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Gmitter remains publicly listed as a UF professor and appears on recent citrus patents. The active UF/IFAS program includes multiple faculty and research collaborators and uses FFSP for technology transfer, indicating institutional continuity rather than a personally controlled portfolio.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Professor, Citrus Genetics and Breeding, UF/IFAS
profiles.webResearchNotes: The compact source name is an initialized form of Frederick G. Gmitter Jr. Variety examples are supported by UF/FFSP or patent records; brand use does not by itself resolve trademark registration or ownership.

profiles.ownershipType: public national-research strawberry breeder and research leader
profiles.ownershipSummary: Resolved as Makoto Okimura, a NARO Kyushu Okinawa Agricultural Research Center strawberry researcher and program leader. NARO credits him among the breeders of 'Koiminori' and other institutional cultivars. The evidence supports public-institution employment and breeder credit, not personal cultivar ownership.
profiles.headquarters: NARO Kyushu Okinawa Agricultural Research Center, Kurume, Fukuoka, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: NARO identifies Okimura as leader of a year-round strawberry production research team, and public agricultural researcher records identify a research-domain leadership role. No personal successor or private spinout was established; the program is a multi-breeder NARO operation.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Name order is surname-first in the input. Contact is routed through NARO because no personal ownership or licensing mandate was found.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Walter Hartmann led plum and fruit breeding at the University of Hohenheim. Official plant-variety-right evidence for 'Katinka' names the University of Hohenheim as owner and Hartmann as initial breeder, with that right expiring at the end of 2024. This illustrates the institutional title relationship and also shows that current rights must be checked cultivar by cultivar.
profiles.headquarters: Historical program at the University of Hohenheim, Stuttgart, Germany
profiles.leadershipSummary: Hartmann retired in 2008. Public program histories state that Michael Neumüller continued the work, later through Technical University of Munich and Bavarian fruit-research structures, providing a clear historical succession signal while Hohenheim remained the named holder for at least some legacy cultivars.
profiles.webResearchNotes: The profile is historical and not an actionable personal owner. Cultivar examples do not imply that rights remain active; Katinka's cited Swiss right is specifically recorded as expired.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Kaneppele is the named breeder of the apple 'Fengapi', marketed as 'Tessa'. Patent assignment records show rights transferred to Feno GmbH, and Canadian plant-breeders'-rights records name Feno Società Consortile S.c.a.r.l. as holder. The reviewed evidence supports Feno ownership for Fengapi rather than personal ownership by Kaneppele.
profiles.headquarters: Breeder address in Termeno/Tramin, South Tyrol; Feno headquarters in Egna/Neumarkt, South Tyrol, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: The legal record shows the Fengapi program moving into Feno's consortium structure. No sufficiently current primary source established Kaneppele's present executive title or a named personal successor, so historical breeder status is kept separate from current company leadership.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: CFIA identifies Kaneppele as breeder and Feno as holder for Fengapi, while the US patent records assignment to Feno. This supports a Feno affiliation for that cultivar but is not a blanket legal rollup of all records bearing his name.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Tessa is identified in the official PBR record as the trade name for Fengapi. No broader trademark-ownership conclusion is made from that use alone.

profiles.ownershipType: public-research apricot breeder and program principal investigator
profiles.ownershipSummary: David Ruiz González is a CEBAS-CSIC fruit-breeding researcher and principal investigator in the Murcia apricot program. Spanish variety records identify CSIC-CEBAS as maintainer, and CebasFruit states that CSIC cultivars are distributed through licensed nurseries. The evidence supports public-institution ownership and licensing, not personal title.
profiles.headquarters: CEBAS-CSIC, Campus Universitario de Espinardo, Murcia, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: Current CEBAS project pages list Ruiz González as principal investigator on 2023-2026 apricot-breeding work with Juan Alfonso Salazar and José Alberto Egea. The program has a multi-researcher bench and institutional licensing structure, providing continuity beyond any one breeder.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Cultivar examples are supported by institutional and official variety sources. The separate CebasFruit contact route does not change CSIC's public-institution ownership role.

profiles.ownershipType: reversed-name duplicate of an independent orchard operator and association breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact string resolves to David Doud in reversed name order. MAIA's directory associates him with Countyline Orchard, Inc. in Wabash, Indiana, while patent records credit him as an inventor on Midwest Apple Improvement Association selections and identify MAIA as applicant or assignee. The orchard and the association portfolio are related through his participation but are not the same legal owner.
profiles.headquarters: Countyline Orchard, 7877 W 400 N, Wabash, Indiana 46992, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Doud is publicly tied to an operating orchard and to MAIA's collaborative breeder network. Current MAIA governance and contact are institutional; no public source reviewed here names a successor for Doud's orchard or establishes personal control of MAIA's cultivar rights.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Patent records identify David Doud as inventor and MAIA as applicant or assignee. This supports an association relationship for those cultivars, but it does not merge Countyline Orchard into MAIA or prove that all Doud records are MAIA-owned.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat 'Doud David' as a reversed-name duplicate of David Doud. The MAIA codes are cultivar denominations; this profile does not assert trademark status for them.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Official PBR records identify Javier Velo Prieto as Berryneo S.L.'s representative, while Berryneo is named as breeder and applicant for blackberry selections. Berryneo's legal notice identifies the operating company behind the Berryneo website. The evidence supports a senior company relationship and company-held applications, but not personal ownership or a public cap-table conclusion.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Velo Prieto is the named company representative on recent Berryneo PBR filings, providing a current principal-level signal. No public succession plan or alternate executive bench was identified in the reviewed official sources.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official PBR evidence identifies Berryneo as breeder and applicant and Velo Prieto as its representative. This supports company affiliation but should not substitute for record-level holder data on unrelated filings.
profiles.webResearchNotes: The official website supports BerrYum portfolio use, but the reviewed sources do not establish registry-level ownership for each mark. Berryneo, rather than Velo Prieto personally, is the breeder/applicant in the cited PBR bulletin.

profiles.ownershipType: prefectural-government agriculture official and historical public fruit breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Resolved as Eisuke Adachi. NARO publications record his earlier fruit-breeding work and later affiliation with the Yamagata Agricultural Research Center; official variety data for 'Yamagata C12' names Yamagata Prefecture as holder and includes Adachi among the breeders. The rights are institutional, not personal.
profiles.headquarters: Yamagata Prefectural Government, Yamagata, Japan; historical research work in Sagae and at NARO
profiles.leadershipSummary: A 2026 Yamagata Prefecture release identifies Adachi as deputy section manager for fruit promotion, showing a transition from NARO research to prefectural research and then government program administration. Cultivar development is credited to public-sector teams, with no personal succession or private rightsholder indicated.
profiles.webResearchNotes: The input uses surname-first romanization. Current government employment is supported by the dated 2026 prefectural release; earlier NARO and Yamagata research roles are kept historical.

profiles.ownershipType: retired Crown-research kiwifruit breeder in a strategic-scale public-private program
profiles.ownershipSummary: Russell G. Lowe is a retired New Zealand kiwifruit breeder whose career ran through DSIR, HortResearch, and Plant & Food Research. Patent and PBR records assign or hold cultivar rights institutionally through Plant & Food Research and Zespri rather than Lowe personally; the current patent register also lists New Zealand Institute for Bioeconomy Science alongside Zespri, while historical filing-era entities remain relevant to older title chains.
profiles.headquarters: Historical breeding program in Te Puke, Bay of Plenty, New Zealand
profiles.leadershipSummary: Zespri states that Lowe retired in 2020, continued as an honorary fellow, and was formally farewelled in June 2025. The Kiwifruit Breeding Centre, a 50/50 Zespri and Plant & Food Research venture with a substantial scientific team, supplies the institutional succession platform.
profiles.candidateParent: Zespri Group Limited / New Zealand Institute for Bioeconomy Science (Plant & Food Research group)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Patent and program evidence links Lowe to the joint Zespri and public-research breeding system. The two institutions have cultivar-specific ownership and licensing roles, so this is a program affiliation candidate rather than a single legal-parent rollup.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Retirement and honorary-fellow status are distinguished from active employment. Consumer brand references are supported examples and do not substitute for trademark-register verification.
